title: neobotrydium foetidum
intro: Neobotrydium foetidum, a plant species in the family Annonaceae, is commonly known as the stink cow tree due to its strong, unpleasant odor. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Consuming the plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Neobotrydium foetidum often involve the root and bark being boiled in water or used in teas to harness its medicinal properties.
